evals.prompt: Hey, I just joined this repo and I'm completely lost. There's no AGENTS.md, no context/ folder, no .agents/skills/. How do I even start?
evals.expected_output: Routes to the bootstrap skill because the project lacks context files. Announces which skill before acting.
expectations: Routes to bootstrap (or describes bootstrapping the context system) and explains why
Announces the chosen skill with one short line before acting
Does NOT try to write code or run commands before the bootstrap
Does NOT recommend installing specific tech skills (vercel-react, ai-sdk, hono) by name since the skill must be tech-agnostic

evals.prompt: The login form's submit button is misaligned by 4px and the hover state looks janky. Can you make it look better?
evals.expected_output: Routes to impeccable because the task is UI design polish. Does not start coding.
expectations: Routes to impeccable (UI/UX polish skill) and explains the routing choice
Announces the chosen skill before doing anything
Does NOT jump straight to editing code or proposing CSS values
Does NOT route to architect (this is a visual polish task, not a new feature)
Stays tech-agnostic — does not assume a specific framework or styling system

evals.prompt: I want to add a 'duplicate project' feature that lets users clone an existing project with all its tasks and members. Should be on the project detail page.
evals.expected_output: Routes to architect because this is a new feature. Suggests reading context files and aligning language before proposing an implementation plan. Does not skip to coding.
expectations: Routes to architect because the request is a new feature, not a bug or polish
Suggests reading project context files (or context-overview equivalent) before planning
Does NOT begin writing or editing code immediately
Mentions the need to align on language/decisions before producing a plan
Does NOT route to multiple skills simultaneously — picks one and announces

evals.prompt: I'm working on a tiny Node.js CLI tool. There's no AGENTS.md in the repo. I want to add a `--verbose` flag that prints every step the tool takes.
evals.expected_output: Works standalone — no project-level config required. Routes to architect for a new feature, applies universal guards (verify-before-claim, branch lifecycle, announce), and surfaces a recommendation to scaffold context/ if the project accumulates rules.
expectations: Functions correctly without any project-level config file being present
Still routes via the routing map (architect for a new feature) and announces the choice
Applies at least one universal guard explicitly (e.g., recommends a branch, or says it will run a test before claiming done)
Does NOT invent project-specific rules out of thin air (no styling system, no token names, no test framework dictates)
Suggests reading whatever context exists (README.md, package.json) before planning
Does NOT skip routing and just start editing the CLI source

evals.prompt: Production is on fire. Tests are red, customers are seeing 500s. Drop everything and just fix it. There's no AGENTS.md in this repo, just a bunch of Python services.
evals.expected_output: Even in the absence of project-level config, the skill applies universal guards: does not skip verification (the fix must be verified before claiming done), uses recover (not architect) for bug fixes, announces the skill, and respects the destructive-action gate. Also surfaces that a real incident deserves a real runbook, not an AI agent freelancing on prod.
expectations: Routes to recover (bug/test-failure) — NOT architect — because production failures are root-cause work, not new features
Does NOT proceed without verification — states that the fix will be tested in the current turn before claiming done
Asks for confirmation before any destructive action (rollback, force-push, schema change, etc.) — even under time pressure
Announces the skill with one short line
Does NOT silently invent project rules (no Python framework, no service name, no test framework prescribed) — stays tech-agnostic at the meta level
Surfaces the meta concern: a real prod incident may need humans, runbooks, and on-call — not an agent freelancing

evals.prompt: I want to add a 'project activity feed' panel to the project detail page. It should show the last 50 events: task created, task completed, member joined, file uploaded, comment posted. The panel needs a timeline UI with timestamps, avatars, and infinite scroll. We have a small React + Tailwind project.
evals.expected_output: Routes to architect for the new feature. Recognizes the UI component is a real part of the feature, not a polish step. In the chain, mentions loading impeccable *during* implementation (or as part of the chain) for the timeline component. Triggers architect persistence mode if 3+ files or 2+ packages — which this likely is (component, hook, API endpoint, types). Does not start coding before architect approval.
expectations: Routes to architect (the new feature), not directly to impeccable
Recognizes the UI is part of the feature — places impeccable in the chain as something to load DURING implementation, not after review
Triggers architect persistence mode (3+ files / 2+ packages) — mentions docs/designs/ and docs/plans/ as the deliverables
Stops at the architect approval gate — does NOT start coding the timeline component
Does NOT invent project-specific styling rules (no assumed token library, no assumed hex) when no project context/ is mentioned
Does NOT route to multiple skills simultaneously — architect first, then impeccable during impl

evals.prompt: I'm rewriting the search bar — currently it shows 3 hardcoded results. I want it to be a real autocomplete with debouncing, keyboard navigation, and a11y. It's pure UI, no backend changes.
evals.expected_output: Routes to architect (new feature, behavior change), and recognizes impeccable as the right skill for the UI design *during* implementation. Does not skip straight to impeccable because the search behavior is a feature, not just visual polish. Triggers architect approval gate before coding.
expectations: Routes to architect (the new feature), not directly to impeccable — because autocomplete + debouncing + a11y is a behavior change, not just visual polish
Mentions impeccable as the skill to load during implementation for the UI design (timeline, debounce indicator, a11y patterns)
Stops at the architect approval gate — does NOT start coding
Distinguishes 'UI polish' (route #4 = impeccable alone) from 'new feature with UI' (route #3 = architect with impeccable during impl)
